purpose: Phase implementation-group verdicts generated after grouped implementation batches. Use this as a bounded group-repair queue before the normal phase coverage gate.

repairPolicy.blockedMeans: explicit blocker/fallback decision exists; do not loop on this group before phase gate
repairPolicy.completeMeans: linked source/platform authority rows have enough behavior, parity, endpoint, and evidence data

requiredBehaviors: The target content/session repositories keep this phase as pure in-memory seeded data with no file, SAF, MediaStore, or import/export path.
The target repositories keep media references as simple `mediaName` strings without adding viewer or bitmap-processing logic in phase-02.
The phase-02 content/session layer keeps seeded content separate from cross-screen state; no bundle or deeplink logic is implemented in the data source file itself.
The target keeps seeded content in `home_content_repository.cj` and route behavior in feature/app modules rather than mixing navigation surfaces into the data source.
The target keeps seeded content repositories permission-free in phase-02 and adds no request-permission scaffolding.
`session_repository.cj` preserves the same shared in-memory state responsibilities without any permission prompt or protected-access flow.

`SessionRepository.setRestaurant()` snapshots the exact selected restaurant menu into zero-quantity `DetailMenuItemData` entries, `getMenuItems()` returns that current snapshot, and `detail_store.cj` now prefers the selected snapshot before falling back to...
`login_preferences_service.cj` persists login state through `Preferences` without any permission prompt or protected-access flow.
`LoginPreferencesService` preserves the same single-file single-key login preference contract and exposes read/write/toggle helpers over HarmonyOS `Preferences`.

`LoginPreferencesService` preserves the same persisted login-state contract, and phase-02 startup/login/profile consumers only need immediate reads or writes at route boundaries rather than a long-lived observer subscription.
`LoginPreferencesService.toggleLoginState()` and the login/profile stores preserve the same persisted session flip behavior for startup, login success, and logout.
The target keeps repository/service wiring permission-free for phase-02 startup/session/navigation.
`home_content_repository.cj` keeps catalogue loading fully in-memory and permission-free in phase-02.
`SessionRepository` preserves the same shared state responsibilities without any permission prompt or protected-access flow.
`LoginPreferencesService` keeps login persistence inside HarmonyOS `Preferences` without adding any permission prompt or protected-access flow.

`LoginPreferencesService` preserves the same persisted login-state source of truth, and `StartupLaunchService`, `loginStore`, and `profileStore` reuse that shared state at the same startup/login/logout boundaries that the current-phase source observes throu...
`LoginPreferencesService.readLoginState(context)` reads the same persisted login flag that startup uses to choose onboarding or home.
`LoginPreferencesService.toggleLoginState()` preserves the same persisted session flip behavior reused by startup, login success, and logout flows.

Phase-02 repository translation does not need a target analogue for permission handling here because the source result wrapper is permission-free and repository permission rows are already closed on the actual repository files.
acceptanceEvidence: Replace broad endpoints with precise target functions, types, resources, or tests before closing the group.
riskHints: current evidence uses broad shell/root/whole-file endpoints

requiredBehaviors: `FakeData.kt` only defines in-memory sample menus, restaurants, ads, favourites, and cart rows; it does not implement file or storage behavior.
`FakeData.kt` references drawable-backed sample images only; it does not implement bitmap decoding, EXIF handling, zoom, preview, or gallery logic.
`FakeData.kt` itself does not carry intent, URI, bundle, or deeplink behavior; it only supplies seeded content later consumed by screen flows.
`FakeData.kt` contains no toolbar, drawer, menu, tab, or route-command code; it only seeds lists consumed by navigation-aware screens.
`FakeData.kt` contains no permission request or protected API behavior.

requiredBehaviors: `getAds()` immediately returns the fake advertisement list.
`getFoodItems()` immediately returns the fake recommended food/category list.
`getRestaurantFromName(name)` performs an exact-name lookup over the fake restaurant list.
`getRestaurants()` immediately returns the full fake `restaurantList` from `FakeData.kt`.
`HomeRepositoryImpl` only returns fake restaurant, ad, and food lists and does not touch permission-protected APIs.
`toggleLoginState()` flips the persisted login boolean that controls startup and authenticated navigation.
The source exposes login state as a `Flow<Boolean>` backed by DataStore updates.
`LoginRepositoryImpl` only reads and toggles a boolean DataStore preference; it does not request permissions or call protected APIs.
`LoginRepositoryImpl` stores the login toggle in a single boolean preference file/key (`user_login_state_pref` / `user_login_state`).
`getCartItems()` emits the seeded cart items held by the shared repository.
`getLikedRestaurants()` emits the mutable favourites list held by the shared repository.
`getMenuItems()` emits the current restaurant menu items wrapped as cart items with zero quantity.
`getSavedRestaurant()` emits the currently selected restaurant when one has been stored.
`UserDataRepositoryImpl` carries the selected restaurant across screens by storing it before navigation and exposing it back to consumers.
The user-data repository participates in navigation by storing selection state that home/history actions consume before navigating to detail.
`UserDataRepositoryImpl` exposes selected restaurant, menu items, liked restaurants, and cart items through one-shot `Flow` emissions backed by shared mutable state.
`UserDataRepositoryImpl` manages in-memory selected restaurant, favourites, and cart state only; it does not request permissions.
`Result.kt` only defines the repository `Results` sealed class and `successOr` helper; it does not request permissions or touch protected APIs.

requiredBehaviors: `getAds()` returns the seeded advertisement list from the home repository contract.
`getFoodItems()` returns the seeded food-category list from the home repository contract.
`getRestaurantFromName(name)` performs an exact-name lookup over the seeded restaurant catalogue.
`getRestaurants()` returns the full seeded restaurant catalogue from the home repository contract.
`HomeRepository` only declares in-memory catalogue read methods and does not request permissions or access protected platform APIs.
`toggleLoginState()` flips the persisted login boolean that controls startup and authenticated navigation.
`readLoginState` reads the persisted login flag used by startup gating.
The login repository exposes observable login state so startup and authenticated flows can react to the persisted session value.
`LoginRepository` only models login-state read/toggle behavior and does not request permissions or access protected platform APIs.
`getCartItems()` exposes the shared cart snapshot for later cart/detail rendering.
`getLikedRestaurants()` exposes the shared favourites list for later screens.
`getMenuItems()` exposes the selected restaurant menu snapshot for later detail/cart rendering.
`getSavedRestaurant()` exposes the currently selected restaurant for later detail rendering.
Shared repository selection state feeds later navigation into the detail route.
The shared user-data repository exposes observable restaurant/menu/favourites/cart state so multiple screens refresh from the same repository mutations.
`UserDataRepository` only models shared restaurant, menu, favourites, and cart state and does not request permissions or use protected APIs.
acceptanceEvidence: Closed critical/high features must record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence.
Build pass alone is not per-feature verification.
Referenced target tests need to be covered by the latest accepted test gate or explicit verificationEvidence.
Related platform capabilities must cite targetCapabilitySearch and a Cangjie-callable API, verified replacement, fallback blocker, or deferred blocker.
Replace root/shell/whole-file endpoints with precise target functions, types, resource files, or tests before closure.
riskHints: critical/high behavior cannot close on build pass alone
broad shell endpoint risk
related platform capability must be resolved with Cangjie-callable API, replacement, fallback blocker, or deferred evidence

requiredBehaviors: `MainActivity` passes the splash-resolved start-destination string into Compose navigation instead of using external deeplink data.
`onCreate()` is the lifecycle callback that installs splash gating and starts root navigation rendering.
`MainActivity` owns the root navigation shell that renders routes and forwards the bottom-bar/cart surface through `SetupNavigation`.
`MainActivity` observes splash state to decide the root route rendered by the navigation shell.
`MainActivity` manages splash and root navigation only and does not request permissions or handle protected API results.
`MainActivity.onCreate()` installs splash gating, sets Compose content, and initializes the root screen from the splash view-model state.

requiredBehaviors: `SplashViewModel` exposes observable loading and start-destination state that the activity reads during splash gating and startup rendering.
`SplashViewModel` only reads persisted login state to choose the startup route and does not request permissions or use protected APIs.
`SplashViewModel.init` blocks on the persisted login state, sets the start destination, and clears the loading flag before startup rendering continues.

`Extensions.kt` only defines `Double.round(decimals)` and `Long.getTimeInMins()` formatting helpers; it does not request runtime permissions, handle permission results, or access protected platform APIs.

`Navigation.kt` only manages in-app route and shell composition and does not request permissions or handle protected API results.

groups.title: Platform capability: Background work, services, receivers, widgets, and notifications
reasons: background-and-platform-components: platform capability has concrete target evidence
requiredBehaviors: Detected 10 source artifact(s) with terms: notification_widget, notification, remoteviews, platform_component, service, receiver, background_work
Replace Android platform components with HarmonyOS abilities, background tasks, notifications, or documented product-level omissions.
Preserve user-visible background behavior, notification/widget state, scheduling semantics, and lifecycle entry points when in source scope.
acceptanceEvidence: Record targetCapabilitySearch or structured API evidence, targetApiOrLibrary/targetStrategy when available, targetEndpoints, and tests or validation notes.
Direct platform status requires Cangjie-callable API, package, wrapper, FFI, or bridge evidence.
Fallback or deferred status requires a concrete blocker plus fallback/risk/future path.
Referenced tests must be covered by the latest accepted test gate or explicit verificationEvidence.

platformRows.capabilityName: Background work, services, receivers, widgets, and notifications
reasons: platform capability has concrete target evidence

groups.title: Platform capability: Runtime permissions and protected platform access
reasons: runtime-permissions: not-applicable has notes explanation
requiredBehaviors: Detected 87 source artifact(s) with terms: permissions, permission
Map Android runtime permission flows to HarmonyOS permission APIs, preserving denied/empty/retry states.
Request required permission, handle grant/deny, show user-facing blocked state, and avoid accessing protected APIs before authorization.
acceptanceEvidence: Record targetCapabilitySearch or structured API evidence, targetApiOrLibrary/targetStrategy when available, targetEndpoints, and tests or validation notes.
Direct platform status requires Cangjie-callable API, package, wrapper, FFI, or bridge evidence.
Fallback or deferred status requires a concrete blocker plus fallback/risk/future path.
Referenced tests must be covered by the latest accepted test gate or explicit verificationEvidence.
